FAQs
How Long Is Plumbing School?
The length of plumbing school in North Carolina varies depending on the program you choose. Many trade schools and apprenticeship programs combine hands-on training with online plumbing courses, typically taking 1-2 years to complete. Programs like those at SkillCat may offer flexible schedules, allowing you to complete your studies at your own pace. After finishing your diploma or certification, you’ll be ready to pursue a plumbing career and apply for a plumbing license.
What Is the Highest Qualification in Plumbing?
The highest qualification in the plumbing profession is becoming a Master Plumber. This level requires extensive experience, advanced plumbing skills, and passing an exam. Many licensed plumbers achieve this status after completing apprenticeship programs and earning their plumbing certification.
What Are the Basics of Plumbing?
The basics of plumbing involve understanding pipes, plumbing tools, and systems like water flow and drainage. These skills are often taught in an introduction to online plumbing courses or a free online course like an Alison course. You'll learn to read blueprints, handle plumbing pipes and fittings, and follow plumbing codes to ensure safe installations and repairs. Gaining a strong foundation in math and using basic tools are essential to excelling in this field.
How Do I Train to Become a Plumber?
To train as a plumber, you can choose between enrolling in an accredited trade school, completing online plumbing courses, or joining an apprenticeship program.  You'll learn plumbing tools, systems, and skills while working toward your plumbing license or diploma certificate. Most training paths combine hands-on practice with theoretical knowledge.
Can I Teach Myself Plumbing?
While it’s possible to learn basic plumbing skills through resources like a free online course or DIY tutorials, becoming a certified plumbing professional requires formal training. Enrolling in online plumbing courses or participating in an apprenticeship ensures you gain proper knowledge of plumbing codes, pipes, and safety standards. Accredited courses from Ashworth College or Penn Foster provide the structure and accreditation needed to pursue a successful plumbing career.
How Do I Become a Plumber?
Becoming a plumber involves earning a high school diploma (or equivalent), enrolling in a trade school or online plumbing courses, and gaining hands-on experience through an apprenticeship. Accredited programs, like those at SkillCat, can help you earn your diploma or plumbing certification, setting you on the path to becoming a licensed plumber. Once trained, passing a licensing exam is required in most states to work professionally.
